summary: Bloomberg reported Jumia Technologies planned to cut an initial 10% of its roughly 2,000-person workforce as it implemented AI across departments including operations, logistics, finance, and marketing.
evidenceQuote: Jumia Technologies AG is planning to cut an initial 10% of its workforce of about 2,000 people as the African e-commerce giant implements artificial intelligence across its departments.
notes: Layoffs count is calculated from Bloomberg's reported initial 10% reduction from a workforce of about 2,000 people. Because the CEO directly tied the cuts to implementing AI across departments, this is classified as explicit AI cited.

sources.name: Bloomberg
sources.quote: Jumia Technologies AG is planning to cut an initial 10% of its workforce of about 2,000 people as the African e-commerce giant implements artificial intelligence across its departments.
